objective: Prove a manually triggered, isolated Production TOPT capture and read-only DataHub query loop without enabling a recurring scheduler or claiming the seven-module Production shadow release.
claim_ceiling: Registration only: no code, persistent runtime, source activation, Production credential use, scheduler, query-provider binding, data-readiness, or #53 completion claim.

requirements: an accepted Production-scoped D4 DataHub handoff explicitly names this batch and its allowed environment
accepted D5 capture-control persistence/runtime handoff and accepted D6 confidence/provenance handoff
accepted #171 bounded TOPT Staging evidence and #41 stable DataHub read handoff
accepted #52 exact-release/recovery readiness, #60 Production source-rights/budget approval, and #61 frozen applicability/SLO policy
a preparation revision freezes the immutable TOPT UniverseRef, source policy, production destination, manual command, query fixtures, reviewer, paths, and rollback before implementation

later_claim_dependencies: A new accepted D4 handoff must authorize this exact batch in Production; the current D4 handoffs authorize Local/CI consumers or D3 in Staging only.
D5 must first publish its accepted E4 capture-control handoff; this batch must not duplicate its migrations, run/work/attempt persistence, retry, checkpoint, or recapture paths.
D6 must publish accepted confidence/provenance persistence and indexed traversal evidence before this batch makes trusted metadata or trace claims.
#171 must publish accepted bounded TOPT Staging evidence before Production source activation.
#41 must publish an accepted stable DataHub read handoff before downstream query-provider or consumer-contract claims.
#52, #60, and #61 must be accepted before Production source calls, release binding, or readiness thresholds.
#53 remains open: this batch proves only the manual TOPT slice and cannot claim a seven-module shadow canary, scheduled operation, or Production graduation.

corpus.environment: No execution corpus is authorized during queued registration.
required_strata: one immutable TOPT UniverseRef with 20 issuers, 21 instruments, and 84 required obligations
an explicit operator-triggered Production run with no registered schedule, sensor, or cron
idempotent same-byte rerun and append-only changed-vintage behavior
bounded capture-status, run-metadata, forward-trace, and reverse-trace queries
negative authorization for raw/staging reads, raw-byte responses, arbitrary SQL, mutable universe resolution, and any scheduler activation

source_ceiling: No live source call, Production credential, Vault read, release binding, or scheduler activation is authorized until the listed handoffs and Production approvals are accepted.

release_activation.reason: Queued registration permits neither Production runtime selection nor any schedule, source route, release, or consumer binding.

negative_controls: Production source call, credential use, release binding, or scheduler activation before all named handoffs and approvals
reuse of a Local/CI or D3-Staging-only D4 handoff for this Production batch
changes to D5-owned control-plane migrations, persistence, retry, checkpoint, or recapture paths
direct raw/staging downstream access, raw-byte return, arbitrary SQL, or mutable universe resolution
claiming #53 completion, all-seven-module operation, scheduled execution, or Production graduation

allowed_evidence_readers: issue #271 planning only until a later accepted handoff names a consumer

invalidation: A changed TOPT universe, source/parser/mapping policy, DataHub handoff scope, D5/D6 handoff, query contract, Production destination, release binding, or SLO policy requires a new prepared revision before implementation.
Any attempt to enable a schedule, broad universe, source route, shared migration, or consumer surface outside this batch requires separate authorization and path ownership.
rollback: Revert only the future isolated D8 implementation and its evidence after a prepared revision; never delete or overwrite captured raw, normalized, attempt, provenance, or prior vintage records.
